free_text: Measurements of activation cross sections on
spallation reactions for 59Co and nat-Cu at incident
neutron spectra at Ep=43 and 68 MeV given
H2+ ions beams were injected into the 10 mm
127-mm diameter and 127 mm long BC501A liquid
Protons emitted from the radiator by n-p scattering
are detected by the dE-E counter telescope in a well
defined geometry. The dE detector is used for particle
identification. The dE-detector is a large area
(900 mm2) transmission-type Si detector with a
depletion layer of 150 or 300 mu-m. The E-detector is
a NaI(Tl) crystal (5-cm diam by 3-cm thick) coupled
.Neutron spectrum width is of the order of several
percent. Neutron fluence is of the order
Peak detection efficiency of Ge detector
Data are taken from Table 6 of NST,36,29
SM, after MEMO CP-E089. JPNJCL to JPNIPC changed
